description:
Enriches the OData.Demo example service with AI-facing descriptions and deprecation annotations.

Patch data for OData targets (edmx / csdl-json) is expressed in CSDL JSON annotation format:
https://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata-csdl-json/v4.01/odata-csdl-json-v4.01.html
Annotation keys use the @TermName convention (e.g. @Core.Description).
This applies to both XML (edmx) and JSON (csdl-json) target files. When the target is EDMX XML,
the overlay merge implementation converts the CSDL JSON patch to equivalent <Annotation> elements.

data.@Core.Description: Represents a person or organisation that purchases goods or services.
data.@Core.LongDescription: A Customer is the central entity for managing purchasing relationships. It holds personal contact details, address information, and links to orders and sales records.

data.@Core.Description: Name of the customer's country. Consider navigating to the related Country entity for structured ISO code access instead of relying on this denormalized field.
Date of birth of the customer contact person. This field contains personal data and must be handled in accordance with applicable data privacy regulations.

@Core.Revisions.Description: Fax communication is no longer supported. Use Phone or EmailAddresses instead.

data.@Core.Description: A sellable item offered by the company. Products are organised by category and supplied by one vendor.
URL of a product thumbnail image. Rendered by UI components to visually represent the product in listings and detail views.
A formal request by an employee for approved time off, subject to manager approval or rejection.
Approves a pending leave request. The first bound parameter is the LeaveRequest instance to approve. The requesting employee is notified upon completion.
Rejects a pending leave request. A mandatory rejection reason must be provided and is communicated back to the employee.
